mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable inflation indicates robust consumer spending without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in >60% of co-occurring positive RSAFS and flat CPIAUCSL observations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power in >50% of recovery regime windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals deteriorating construction activity and reduced household wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when HOUST drops below 1200, or the threshold signal shows no predictive edge over a baseline mean-reversion model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate forces monetary policy to remain restrictive, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses explanatory power when CPIAUCSL rises with stable FEDFUNDS, or forward returns are positive in >55% of these joint observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during mid-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle periods, or the signal produces negative returns in >50% of mid-cycle observations.
